title: 奉命谋杀
summary:
Philip Darland and his wife, Mary (also called Polly), continue their argument regarding the murder that occurred in their house. Philip expresses a desire to treat the investigation as an intellectual "murder game" to distract himself. While Mary wants to return to her household duties and forget the event, Philip rejects the idea that an outsider committed the crime, suggesting instead that the killer is someone within the house. He notes that while Old Marshall attempts to provide a polite explanation for the crime, no one believes him.

Philip discusses various motives and suspects. He mentions that for two years, Mary and others believed Jacqueline was the killer. He then turns his attention to the deteriorating relationship between Mary's father, Leo, and his partner, Gwendolyn Vance. Philip suggests their growing distance is due to either mutual suspicion or guilt; he posits that if Gwendolyn is innocent, she faces the torture of being shunned, whereas Leo faces the agony of suspecting the woman he loves. Philip also speculates that the police might suspect Leo of pushing his wife, though he does not personally believe it.

Finally, Philip questions whether Mary is avoiding the truth because she already knows the killer. He proposes a theory regarding Hester, suggesting her motive could stem from long-term suppressed rebellion against her mother, who made Hester acutely aware of her own perceived weaknesses. Philip believes he could construct a justification for Hester's actions.
